body.title: Move loguru/stdlib-intercept ownership from Igor to the base (common-environment logger)
body.intention: I intend that loguru/common-logging is owned by the shared base every device boots into, not by Igor — so any device process gets stdlib→loguru routing regardless of whether Igor is up.
body.description:
**Problem:** `devices/igor/igor_base.py` imported loguru directly and installed the stdlib→loguru `InterceptHandler` globally at module import — residue from when Igor was its own repo. That made a base substrate primitive (common logging) owned and gated by one device. Igor is not special; each device is its own process joining a common environment, and the logger belongs to that common environment (the base), not to Igor.

**Shape:** Extract `InterceptHandler` + install into `diagnostic_base/logging_bridge.py` (the base already owns loguru via TaggedLogger/DiagnosticBase). Install fires once per process from `DiagnosticBase.__init__` (first device boot), NOT at import — so tests/CLI that merely import the base aren't clobbered, and every device process self-establishes common logging regardless of boot order or whether Igor/ground-loop is involved. The ground loop stays lightweight (no loguru dependency — it's the resilience anchor). Igor keeps a backward-compat re-export of `InterceptHandler` only.

**Affected files:** diagnostic_base/logging_bridge.py (new), diagnostic_base/base.py (install in __init__, once-guarded), devices/igor/igor_base.py (drop direct loguru import + install; re-export only).
**Design rules:** oop-first (base owns the contract; devices consume); no behavior change to the ~80 igor_base importers; ground loop stays dependency-light.
**Scope boundary:** IN — relocate the loguru intercept ownership to the base + boot-time (not import-time) per-process install. OUT — routing the rack/agentctl or web-interface processes through the base logger (separate follow-up); the system_alarms work.
**Test plan:** the ~80-importer contract survives (tests/igor/test_igor_base.py, test_agent_base.py, test_audit_logging.py); base + ground-loop suites stay green (tests/test_diagnostic_base.py, test_tagged_logger.py, test_ground_loop*.py); intercept installs on first DiagnosticBase instantiation and NOT on bare import; no `from loguru` import remains under devices/igor/.
**Completion criteria:** grep finds zero `from loguru`/`import loguru` under devices/igor/; importing diagnostic_base.base does NOT install the intercept; instantiating a DiagnosticBase device DOES install it (InterceptHandler on root); the named test files pass unchanged.

[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)

body.result: Moved loguru/stdlib-intercept ownership Igor→base; installs per-process at device boot (covers rack via Skeleton + every device process), ground loop stays lightweight. Proof: tests/test_logging_bridge_ownership.py @ 031295ea.
